pattern: Answering 'which value, in array order, is both even and singular' is a two-pass counting problem: tally every value's frequency once, then walk the array again and stop at the first even value whose tally is exactly one.
hints: First tally how many times every value occurs across the whole array.
Then scan the array in its original order (not by value) so 'first' means earliest index, not smallest value.
Return the first value in that scan that is even and whose total tally is exactly 1; if the scan finishes with none, return -1.

edge_inputs.description: two even candidates, earlier index wins
the only even value is duplicated -- no answer
single element, odd -- no even candidate at all
an odd value appears exactly once but must be ignored -- only evenness matters
the unique even value is not the first even value encountered
all values identical and even -- never unique
every value distinct, first even one wins even though later evens also qualify

stress.description: random values from a small alphabet so duplicates and evenness both occur often
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 12) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
